item.title: 副作用 | Side Effect
item.description:
副作用的示例包括获取请求、直接操作 DOM、使用诸如 setTimeout() 之类的计时器函数等等。



原文:

Tổng hợp từ nhiều nguồn:
voz, viblo.asia

Phải hiểu cốt lõi side effect không chỉ có ở reactjs mà nó có ở bất cứ ngôn ngữ lập trình nào.

Ví dụ đơn giản hàm một hàm submitLogin. Sau khi user nhập username và pass xong nhấn submit thì sẽ trigger hàm submitLogin. Trong hàm submitLogin này sẽ làm những việc này

1./ Submit thông tin lên server và nhận thông tin trả về

2./ Nếu trạng thái thành công redirect sang Dashboard

3./ Nếu trạng thái thất bại sẽ hiển thị lỗi

Ở đây theo như tên gọi submitLogin thì công việc chính của nó là (1). (2) và (3) là side effect vì nó không phải là nhiệm vụ chính của hàm và phải dựa vào kết quả từ (1)

  • Vậy side effect trong react là gì. Bạn có thể hiểu đơn giản side effect là những hành động event có thể làm thay đổi DOM trong react components. Đơn giản như bạn nhập click vào một button thì dữ liệu sẽ fetching về làm thay đổi components bên trong đó hay khi bạn tạo tài khoản thì dữ liệu của bạn sẽ được update và tạo mới trên server chẳng hạn.

  • Khác với việc không có side effect bạn nhập vào gì thì sẽ hiển thị ra như vậy và không có tác động gì tới bên ngoài. Hay bạn cũng có thể hiểu đơn giản bạn có một thẻ input khi bạn thay đổi giá trị input nó sẽ không những thay đổi giá trị bên trong nó mà còn thay đổi giá trị của những biến giá trị hay components khác ngoài nó

  • Vậy hooks liên quan gì tới side effect. Nếu bạn nào theo dõi thì cũng đã biết một tính năng mới mà hooks giới thiệu đó chính là useEffect một function kết hợp 3 trạn thái life cycle trong react là mount, update và unmount và mình tin đây cũng là tính năng làm hooks hết sức nổi bật trong mắt các lập trình viên theo dõi React cũng như Front End

Examples of side-effects are fetch requests, manipulating DOM directly, using timer functions like setTimeout(), and more.
item.author: buiminh15

item.title: #14：Show & Tell：一大群应用程序！ | #14: Show & Tell: A SWArm of Apps!
item.description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA的Week 2, Day 7天！

我们做到了第 2 周结束！！ 让我们为自己鼓掌！

在上周，我们浏览了包含各种应用程序、静态站点生成器和前端框架的使用示例子集。 这仍然只是可能的一小部分。 那么，您如何使这一旅程更具可操作性并在实践中学习呢？

这就是我将在今天的帖子中尝试回答的问题。 也收藏这个，因为我们将在下周末重新访问它（“开发工具”），并尝试使用Azure 静态 Web 应用程序 CLI （本地命令行工具）提供的更简化的体验复制其中一些教程发展）。

我们将涵盖的内容

  • 回顾：使用示例周
  • 构建：如何获得项目创意
  • 分享: 为画廊做贡献
  • 简化：使用静态 Web 应用 CLI！
  • 练习：构建、部署和自定义一些入门应用程序。

第 2 周回顾

本周充满了不同的使用场景和前端技术。 让我们快速浏览一下所涵盖的内容：

视觉路线图让您了解每天所涵盖的内容。 但这只是冰山一角。 而且，正如我们在 Blazor WebAssembly 中看到的那样，非 JavaScript 开发人员还有其他用于开发现代 Web 应用程序的框架。 那么，如何才能继续边做边学的旅程呢？ 继续阅读以了解一些想法。

项目构想

最简单的入门方法是使用当今可用的众多静态站点生成器之一。 其中大多数都提供了您可以开始使用的模板，并通过引入您自己的内容（数据、媒体资产等）进行自定义。 需要一些灵感吗？ 看看最近发布的 JavaScript 新星：静态站点列表，看看这些项目中是否有可以重新利用的启动器。

示例：我的“食谱我”项目。

本周早些时候，我们在使用示例中介绍了 Astro (#2)、Docusarus (#3) 和 VuePress (#9) 的示例。 今天我们来看看十一（＃7）。

今年早些时候，我运行了#30DaysOfPWA ，这是一个类似的系列，专注于渐进式 Web 应用程序。 我想要一个演示！ 我一直想有一个食谱博客，我发现这个 Eleventy 开胃菜非常完美。 我对其进行了定制，以添加使其符合 PWA 所需的功能和代码。 我将其配置为托管在 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序上。 奖励：我在这个过程中学到了很多关于框架的知识，以及 SWA 的配置更改！

相关资源：

  • 阅读教程
  • 浏览源
  • 访问网站

下一步是查看针对流行前端和全栈框架的现有应用程序入门工具包或教程。 与静态站点生成器（以内容为中心）不同，这些应用程序可让您探索可以从 SWA 概念（如无服务器 API 和身份验证支持）中受益的高级用户交互。 需要一些灵感吗？ 看看最近发布的 JavaScript 新星：前端框架列表，看看这些项目中是否有可以重新利用的启动器。

示例：Simona 的“贴纸店”项目。

查看 Simona Cotin（目前是静态 Web 应用团队的首席 PM）的这个示例，它分叉了Strapi 电子商务启动器，并对其进行了调整以部署在 Azure 静态 Web 应用上。 奖励： Simona 的部署还利用了 SWA 的自定义域支持，使应用程序可以在https://aswa.cloud域中访问。

相关资源：

  • 阅读教程
  • 浏览源
  • 访问网站

有时，您找到合适的应用程序并不是因为框架——而是因为它是一个真正引起你共鸣的用例——即使你不一定熟悉底层技术。 这些项目很棒，因为您对最终结果的兴趣使您更有兴趣深入挖掘，提高您对新应用程序架构和工具的理解。 需要一些灵感吗？ 我最喜欢的起点是awesome repo，它是给定技术XYZ的awesome-XYZ列表的目录。 其中许多列表确定了可以作为实验基础的教程、开源项目和入门模板存储库！

示例：我的“Google Codelabs”项目。

如果您参加过任何 Google 开发者活动或社区会议，那么您很可能会遇到他们的Codelabs网站——一个涵盖各种技术的分步教程库。 但是您知道他们开源了 codelabs 管理和托管工具吗？

底层工具使用 Golang，但实际内容是用 Markdown 编写的，并由“代码实验室作为工具”实用程序claat转换为所需的静态文件。 跟随创建您自己的代码实验室 - 并将它们托管在 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序上。 奖励：托管应用程序是有关如何创建它的教程。 这不是元数据吗？

相关资源：

  • 浏览源
  • 访问网站

分享：图库

我们已经讨论了通过重新利用他人生成的开源项目、教程或模板来帮助您学习的项目创意。 现在让我们谈谈通过分享您的项目来激励他人来支付它。

这就是我们将在 5 月底之前推出的静态 Web 应用程序展示的目的。 您可以预览应用程序库的外观，允许开发人员搜索具有实时部署和开源代码库的项目，并使用列出的前端技术之一。 将此帖子添加为书签或跟踪此站点上的展示链接，以了解展示功能何时上线！ 成为第一个为该列表中添加的一些最新技术提交项目的人！

简化：SWA CLI

最后一件事。 在到目前为止的所有使用示例和教程中，我们通常强调IDE选项（使用 Visual Studio Code SWA 扩展）或Azure 门户选项（由 repo README 上配置的Deploy to Azure按钮激活）。

但是，如果您可以从命令行执行所有操作——从初始化项目配置，到在本地构建和测试应用程序，再到在生产或预览环境中将其部署到云中，该怎么办？ 这就是Azure 静态 Web 应用 CLI背后的承诺。 加入我们，参加 5 月 19 日的特别#SWAanniversary 活动，在此背景下发布一些令人兴奋的公告。

然后在 5 月晚些时候加入书签并重新访问此页面以获取更新的部分，我们将在其中讨论如何使用此工具进一步简化和简化“边做边学”项目！

锻炼

我们查看了您可以开始使用的各种资源，以构建一个真实世界的应用程序，然后您可以配置该应用程序以进行 SWA 部署和进一步的自定义。 继续选择一些东西并扩展应用程序以创建新的东西。 然后将其贡献给我们即将推出的 Showcase项目！

需要更多灵感？ 我是 React 的粉丝，但我想探索Preact作为具有可比 API 的高性能替代品。 试试看！



原文:

Welcome to Week 2, Day 7 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

We made it to the end of Week 2!! Let's give ourselves a round of applause!

In the last week we walked through a subset of usage examples featuring diverse applications, static site generators and front-end frameworks. And that's still just a small slice of what is possible. So how do you make this journey more actionable and learn by doing?

That's what I'll try to answer in today's post. Also bookmark this one because we will revisit it at the end of next week ("Dev Tools") and try to replicate some of these tutorials using the more streamlined experience provided by the Azure Static Web Apps CLI (a command-line tool for local development).

What We'll Cover

  • Recap: Usage Examples Week
  • Build: How to Get Project Ideas
  • Share: Contribute to the Gallery
  • Simplify: Using Static Web Apps CLI!
  • Exercise: Build, Deploy, and Customize, some starter app.

Week 2 Recap

This week was packed with different usage scenarios and front-end technologies. Let's take a quick look at what was covered:

The visual roadmap gives you a sense of what was covered each day. But this is just the tip of the iceberg. JavaScript developers have a wide variety of front-end frameworks and static site generators to work with. And, as we saw with Blazor WebAssembly, non-JavaScript developers have other frameworks they use to develop modern web applications. So, how can you continue the learn-by-doing journey? Read on for some ideas.

Project Ideas

1. Static Sites

The easiest way to get started is to use one of many static site generators available today. Most of these provide templates you can start with, and customize just by bringing your own content (data, media assets etc.). Need some inspiration? Take a look at the recently released Rising Stars Of JavaScript: Static Sites list and see if any of those projects have starters you can repurpose.

Example: My "Recipe Me" Project.

Earlier this week, we covered examples with Astro (#2), Docusarus (#3) and VuePress (#9) in our usage examples. Today we look at Eleventy (#7).

Earlier this year I ran #30DaysOfPWA, a similar series focused on Progressive Web Apps. And I wanted a demo! I've always wanted to have a recipe blog, and I found this Eleventy starter that was perfect. I customized it to add the features and code required to make it PWA-compliant. And I configured it for hosting on Azure Static Web Apps. Bonus: I learned a lot in the process about the framework, and the configuration changes for SWA!

Related Resources:

  • Read the Tutorial
  • Browse the Source
  • Visit the Website

2. Frameworks

The next step up from this is to look at existing application starter-kits or tutorials that target popular front-end and full-stack frameworks. Unlike static site generators (which are content-centric) these apps let you explore advanced user interactions that can benefit from SWA concepts like serverless API and authentication support. Need some inspiration? Take a look at the recently released Rising Stars Of JavaScript: Front-end Frameworks list and see if any of those projects have starters you can repurpose.

Example: Simona's "Sticker Shop" Project.

Check out this example from Simona Cotin (currently a Principal PM on the Static Web Apps team) which forked the Strapi e-commerce starter and adapted it for deployment on Azure Static Web Apps. The Strapi tutorial talks you through the setup and structure of the application - making it a perfect place to explore just adding SWA integrations. Bonus: Simona's deployment also takes advantage of SWA's custom domain support, making the app accessible at the https://aswa.cloud domain.

Related Resources:

  • Read the Tutorial
  • Browse the Source
  • Visit the Website

3. OSS Apps

Sometimes, you find the right application not because of the framework - but because its a use case that really resonates with you - even if you are not necessarily familiar with the underlying technology. These projects are great because your interest in the end result makes you more curious to dig under the hood, improving your understanding of new applicaton architectures and tooling. Need some inspiration? My favorite starting point is the awesome repo which is a directory of awesome-XYZ lists for a given technology XYZ. Many of these lists identify tutorials, open-source projects and starter template repos that can be the basis for your experiments!

Example: My "Google Codelabs" Project.

If you've attended any Google Developer events or community conferences, chances are you'll have encountered their Codelabs site - a repository of step-by-step tutorials that cover a wide range of technologies. But did you know that they open-sourced the codelabs management and hosting tools?

The underlying tooling uses Golang but the actual content is written in Markdown, and converted into the required static files by claat, the "Code Labs As A Tool" utility. Follow along to create your own codelabs - and host them on Azure Static Web Apps. Bonus: The hosted app is the tutorial for how it was created. Isn't that meta?

Related Resources:

  • Read the Tutorial
  • Browse the Source
  • Visit the Website

Share: Gallery

We've talked about Project ideas that help you learn by repurposing the open-source projects, tutorials or templates generated by others. Now let's talk about paying it forward by sharing your project to inspire others.

That is the purpose of the Static Web Apps Showcase that we will be debuting before the end of May. You can see a sneak preview of what the app gallery looks like, allowing developers to search for projects that have live deployments and open source codebases, and use one of the listed front-end technologies. Bookmark this post or track the Showcase link on this site, to know when the Showcase feature goes live! Be the first to submit a project for some of the more recent technologies added to that list!

Simplify: SWA CLI

One last thing. In all the usage examples and tutorial so far, we've typically emphasized either the IDE option (using the Visual Studio Code SWA extension) or the Azure Portal option (activated by the Deploy to Azure button configured on repo README).

But what if you could do everything from the command-line - from initializing your project configuration, to building and testing your application locally, to deploying it to the cloud in a production or preview environment? That's the promise behind the Azure Static Web Apps CLI. Join us May 19 for a special #SWAanniversary event for some exciting announcements in this context.

Then bookmark and revisit this page later in May for an updated section where we discuss how we can further simplify and streamline our "learn by doing" projects with this tool!

Exercise

We've looked at various resources you can start with, to scaffold a real-world application that you can then configure for SWA deployment and further customization. Go ahead and pick something and expand on the app to create something new. Then contribute it to our upcoming Showcase project!

Need more inspiration? I'm a fan of React, but I've wanted to explore Preact as a performant alternative with a comparable API. The Preact Demos page has a rich set of examples - including this Homepage Generator which is a perfect 1-page resume template you can customize for your profile. Try it out!
item.author: nitya

item.title: 误差边界 | Error Boundaries
item.description:
错误边界 là các 组件 React bắt lỗi JavaScript ở bất kỳ đâu trong cây 组件 con của chúng, ghi lại các lỗi đó và hiển thị giao diện người dùng dựịủ thay vì cây 组件错误边界 bắt lỗi trong quá trình 渲染，trong các phương thức vòng đời và trong các hàm tạo của toàn bộ cây bên dưới chúng。

错误边界 không bắt lỗi đối với：

  • 事件处理程序（了解更多）
  • 异步代码（例如 setTimeout 或 requestAnimationFrame 回调）
  • 服务器端渲染
  • 在错误边界本身（而不是其子项）中引发的错误

类组件 sẽ trở thành 错误边界 nếu nó định nghĩa một trong hai (hoặc cả hai) phương thức vòng đời static getDerivedStateFromError () hoặc componentDidCatch () 。 错误边界không thể tự bắt lỗi。 演示

例子

组件堆栈跟踪

错误边界không bắt lỗi bên trong事件处理程序。

反应 không cần错误边界để khôi phục từ lỗi trong事件处理程序。 Không giống như phương thức render và phương thức lifecycle , Event Handlers không xảy ra trong quá trình rendering. Không giống như phương thức 渲染 và phương thức 生命周期，事件处理程序không xảy ra trong quá trình 渲染。 Vì vậy, nếu event handler ném ra lỗi, React vẫn biết những gì sẽ hiển thị trên màn hình. Vì vậy，nếu事件处理程序ném ra lỗi，React vẫn biết những gì sẽ hiển thị trên màn hình。

Nếu bạn cần bắt lỗi bên trong Event Handlers , hãy sử dụng câu lệnh try / catch JavaScript thông thường: Nếu bạn cần bắt lỗi bên trong事件处理程序，hãy sử dụng câu lệnh try / catch JavaScript thường：

 class MyComponent extends React.Component { constructor(props) { super(props); this.state = { error: null }; this.handleClick = this.handleClick.bind(this); } handleClick() { try { // Do something that could throw } catch (error) { this.setState({ error }); } } render() { if (this.state.error) { return <h1>Caught an error.</h1> } return <button onClick={this.handleClick}>Click Me</button> } }

Lưu ý rằng ví dụ trên thể hiện hành vi JavaScript thông thường và không sử dụng các Error Boundary. Lưu ý rằng ví dụ trên thể hiện hành vi JavaScript thông thường và không sử dụng các 错误边界。



原文:

Bài viết dịch từ:
https://reactjs.org/docs/error-boundaries.html

Trước đây, các lỗi JavaScript bên trong các component thường làm hỏng trạng thái bên trong của React và khiến nó phát ra các lỗi khó hiểu trong lần hiển thị tiếp theo. Những lỗi này gây ra do lỗi trước đó trong code gây ra, nhưng React không cung cấp cách xử lý chúng một cách linh hoạt trong các component và không thể khôi phục chúng.

Giới thiệu Error Boundaries

Lỗi JavaScript trong một phần của giao diện người dùng không nên làm hỏng toàn bộ ứng dụng. Để giải quyết vấn đề này cho người dùng React, React 16 giới thiệu một khái niệm mới về “Error Boundaries”.

Error Boundaries là các component React bắt lỗi JavaScript ở bất kỳ đâu trong cây component con của chúng, ghi lại các lỗi đó và hiển thị giao diện người dùng dự phòng thay vì cây component bị lỗi. Error Boundaries bắt lỗi trong quá trình rendering, trong các phương thức vòng đời và trong các hàm tạo của toàn bộ cây bên dưới chúng.

Error Boundaries không bắt lỗi đối với:

  • Event handlers (learn more)
  • Asynchronous code (e.g. setTimeout or requestAnimationFrame callbacks)
  • Server side rendering
  • Errors thrown in the error boundary itself (rather than its children)

Class component sẽ trở thành error boundary nếu nó định nghĩa một trong hai (hoặc cả hai) phương thức vòng đời static getDerivedStateFromError () hoặc componentDidCatch (). Sử dụng static getDerivedStateFromError () để hiển thị một giao diện người dùng dự phòng sau khi một lỗi được đưa ra. Sử dụng componentDidCatch () để ghi thông tin lỗi.

class ErrorBoundary extends React.Component {
  constructor(props) {
    super(props);
    this.state = { hasError: false };
  }

  static getDerivedStateFromError(error) {
    // Update state so the next render will show the fallback UI.
    return { hasError: true };
  }

  componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) {
    // You can also log the error to an error reporting service
    logErrorToMyService(error, errorInfo);
  }

  render() {
    if (this.state.hasError) {
      // You can render any custom fallback UI
      return <h1>Something went wrong.</h1>;
    }

    return this.props.children; 
  }
}

Sau đó, bạn có thể sử dụng nó như một component thông thường:

<ErrorBoundary>
  <MyWidget />
</ErrorBoundary>

Các error boundaries hoạt động giống như JavaScript catch{}, nhưng đối với component. Chỉ các class component mới có thể là error boundaries. Trong thực tế, hầu hết thời gian bạn sẽ muốn khai báo ErrorBoundary một lần và sử dụng nó trong toàn bộ ứng dụng của mình.

Lưu ý rằng Error Boundaries chỉ bắt lỗi trong các component bên dưới chúng trong cây. Error Boundary không thể tự bắt lỗi. Nếu một Error Boundary không thể hiển thị thông báo lỗi, lỗi sẽ lan truyền đến Error Boundary gần nhất phía trên nó. Điều này cũng tương tự như cách khối catch {} hoạt động trong JavaScript.

Demo

Example

Nơi đặt Error Boundaries

Mức độ chi tiết của Error Boundary là tùy thuộc vào bạn. Bạn có thể bao bọc các component tuyến cấp cao nhất để hiển thị thông báo "Đã xảy ra sự cố" cho người dùng, giống như cách phía máy chủ thường xử lý các sự cố. Bạn cũng có thể bao bọc các widget riêng lẻ trong một Error Boundary để bảo vệ chúng khỏi làm hỏng phần còn lại của ứng dụng.

Điều này là tùy vào người viết, có thể đặt ở tầng trên cùng của ứng dụng để bắt toàn bộ lỗi, cũng có thể đặt sâu hơn để bắt lỗi cho cụ thể.

Hành vi mới cho các lỗi chưa xảy ra

Sự thay đổi này có một hàm ý quan trọng. Kể từ React 16, các lỗi không nằm trong bất kỳ Error Boundaries nào sẽ dẫn đến việc ngắt kết nối toàn bộ cây component React.

Chúng tôi đã tranh luận về quyết định này, nhưng theo kinh nghiệm của chúng tôi, việc để lại giao diện người dùng bị hỏng tại chỗ còn tệ hơn là xóa bỏ hoàn toàn. Ví dụ: trong một sản phẩm như Messenger, việc hiển thị giao diện người dùng bị hỏng có thể dẫn đến việc ai đó gửi tin nhắn đến nhầm người. Tương tự, ứng dụng thanh toán hiển thị sai số tiền còn tệ hơn là không hiển thị gì.

Thay đổi này có nghĩa là khi bạn chuyển sang React 16, bạn có thể sẽ phát hiện ra các lỗi hiện có trong ứng dụng của mình mà trước đây chưa được chú ý. Thêm Error Boundaries cho phép bạn cung cấp trải nghiệm người dùng tốt hơn khi có sự cố.

Ví dụ: Facebook Messenger bọc nội dung của sidebar, bảng thông tin, nhật ký cuộc trò chuyện và đầu vào tin nhắn thành các Error Boundaries riêng biệt. Nếu một số thành phần trong một trong các khu vực giao diện người dùng này gặp sự cố, phần còn lại của chúng vẫn tương tác.

Chúng tôi cũng khuyến khích bạn sử dụng các dịch vụ báo cáo lỗi JS (hoặc xây dựng của riêng bạn) để bạn có thể tìm hiểu về các trường hợp ngoại lệ không được khắc phục khi chúng xảy ra trong quá trình sản xuất và khắc phục chúng.

Component Stack Traces

React 16 in tất cả các lỗi xảy ra trong quá trình hiển thị cho console ở môi trường phát triển. Ngoài thông báo lỗi và JavaScript stack, nó cũng cung cấp component stack traces. Bây giờ bạn có thể thấy vị trí chính xác lỗi trong cây component:

Bạn cũng có thể xem tên tệp và số dòng trong component stack trace. Điều này hoạt động theo mặc định trong Create React App:

Nếu không sử dụng Create React App, bạn có thể thêm plugin này theo cách thủ công vào cấu hình Babel của mình. Lưu ý rằng nó chỉ dành cho mục đích development và phải bị vô hiệu hóa trong production.

Thế còn try/catch

try / catch dùng cho imperative code

try {
  showButton();
} catch (error) {
  // ...
}

Tuy nhiên, các component React là declarative và chỉ định những gì sẽ được hiển thị:

Tham khảo: declarative vs imperative

Thế còn Event Handlers

Error Boundary không bắt lỗi bên trong Event Handlers.

React không cần Error Boundary để khôi phục từ lỗi trong Event Handlers. Không giống như phương thức render và phương thức lifecycle, Event Handlers không xảy ra trong quá trình rendering. Vì vậy, nếu event handler ném ra lỗi, React vẫn biết những gì sẽ hiển thị trên màn hình.

Nếu bạn cần bắt lỗi bên trong Event Handlers, hãy sử dụng câu lệnh try / catch JavaScript thông thường:

class MyComponent extends React.Component {
  constructor(props) {
    super(props);
    this.state = { error: null };
    this.handleClick = this.handleClick.bind(this);
  }

  handleClick() {
    try {
      // Do something that could throw
    } catch (error) {
      this.setState({ error });
    }
  }

  render() {
    if (this.state.error) {
      return <h1>Caught an error.</h1>
    }
    return <button onClick={this.handleClick}>Click Me</button>
  }
}

Lưu ý rằng ví dụ trên thể hiện hành vi JavaScript thông thường và không sử dụng các Error Boundary.
item.author: buiminh15

item.title: 基于属性的节点测试框架 | Property Based Testing Framework for Node
item.description:
假设的使用非常直观和简单，完美地呈现了基于属性的测试的概念。 所以我也想在 Node.js 中找到一个等效的替代方案。 其中两个在 Github 上有很高的星级评分， JSVerify有 1.6K 星，快速检查有 2.8K 星。 所以我花了一些时间来研究fast-check ，并尝试更接近我的日常工作。

本文是一个回顾，是一个记录经验的简单示例。

为什么要进行基于属性的测试？

在提供示例之前，让我们解释一下为什么我们使用基于属性的测试。 事实上，我不喜欢基于属性的术语。 用我的话来说，“超大量”测试。

我们都知道Test Pyramid如下。

在我之前的文章中，我提到了单元测试和集成测试之间的区别。 在金字塔的较低级别，需要更多的测试用例。

即便如此，也很难生成大量的测试用例。 我们通常根据已知条件或产品规格编写相应的测试，有时我们可能会记得编写边界测试（有时不会），有时我们可能依赖于简单的随机功能验证，例如faker 。

但是，总的来说，即使我们努力想出测试用例，也无法涵盖所有​​场景，我们将这种测试方法称为基于示例的测试。 这是因为我们提出的测试用例基本上是从某个例子扩展而来的，不能覆盖所有未知的上下文，也不能测试所有的边界条件。

此时，我们希望有一个框架自动生成足够多的场景（合理的场景与否）来验证我们编写的代码，而我们编写的测试用例只需要确保它们的“属性”是正确的。 这就是基于属性的测试的起源。

尽管如此

现实情况是集成测试与单元测试大致相同。

我曾在许多组织工作过，从大型国有企业到小型初创公司。 无论我是开发人员还是导师，从过去的经验来看，单元测试与集成测试一样重要。

对于大多数开发人员来说，正确划分单元测试和集成测试并不是一件容易的事。 为了能够完全拆分测试用例，他们需要具备设计模式、依赖注入、依赖反转等技能才能做好。 因此，大多数测试环境都是基于特定的测试环境，比如使用docker-compose生成一次性数据库和测试数据并在其上进行测试。

一般来说，我需要几个能力。

  1. 能够测试异步/等待。
  2. 能够验证更多上下文，例如assertEqual 。

速查简介

在开始编写测试用例之前，我们先来看看fast-check的基本用法。

首先介绍一下fast-check的结构。

  • 断言（fc.assert）
  • 属性（fc.property 或 fc.asyncProperty）

需要这些属性来描述两个重要的块。

另一方面，任意值是目标的输入参数，由属性自动生成，我们所要做的就是为它们提供规则，例如，只有整数。

下面是一个简单的例子。

 fc . assert ( fc . property ( fc . integer (), fc . integer (), ( i , j ) => { return i + j === add ( i , j ); }) );

这两个fc.integer()是任意的，后面的匿名函数是 runner，它有两个参数i和j ，对应前面的任意。 我们要验证函数add是否真的正确地对两个参数求和，所以add的结果应该与+一致。

让我们回顾一下我们刚才提到的两个要求。

  1. 虽然我们的测试目标是add ，但是在 runner 中与一些条件很好的整合，不仅可以做出 boolean 的效果。

快速检查示例

现在让我们来看一个更实际的例子。 假设我有一个数据库表，每个用户都有钱。

用户身份钱
美国广播公司定义有一个函数async function getMoney(limit)将按升序对钱进行排序，并根据参数确定返回多少钱。

现在我们要测试这个黑盒。

 describe ( " fast-check test " , () => { before ( async () => { // generate 10 random records }); it ( " #1 " , async () => { const result = await getMoney ( 100 ); expect ( result . length ). to . be . equal ( 10 ); }); it ( " #2 " , async () => { await fc . assert ( fc . asyncProperty ( fc . integer (), async ( i ) => { const result = await getMoney ( i ); return result . length <= 10 && result . length >= 0 ; }) ); }); it ( " #3 " , async () => { await fc . assert ( fc . asyncProperty ( fc . integer ({ min : 0 , max : 10 }), async ( i ) => { const result = await getMoney ( i ); return result . length === i ; }) ); }); it ( " #4 " , async () => { await fc . assert ( fc . asyncProperty ( fc . integer (), async ( i ) => { const result = await getMoney ( i ); if ( result . length > 1 ) { let prev = parseFloat ( result [ 0 ]); for ( let i = 1 ; i < result . length ; i ++ ) { const curr = parseFloat ( result [ i ]); if ( curr < prev ) { return false ; } prev = curr ; } } return true ; }) ); }); });

让我简要解释一下。

  1. 只是简单地验证该功能确实有效，没有使用fast-check 。
  2. 给定一个任意整数，返回结果的长度应该在 0 到 10 之间，因为我们在before中只创建了 10 条记录。
  3. 给定一个整数范围，返回的长度应该等于给定的长度。
  4. 验证整个数组的顺序确实是升序。 从这个 runner 可以看出，即使是非常复杂的条件也可以验证，但是要注意不要在测试用例中制造 bug 导致需要测试用例的测试用例。

如果检测到问题， fast-check还会告诉您它使用哪种任意方法来检测问题。 例如，

反例：[-1234567890]

这意味着当i = -1234567890时测试用例失败。 可能未正确处理负数或未正确处理“大”负数。 这是时候编写一个真正的单元测试（或集成测试）并验证-1234567890，以便以后可以将这样的失败案例用作回归测试。

结论

理想情况下，当测试这样的数据库行为时，我们会使用依赖注入等技术来隔离物理数据库，以提高测试性能。 但正如我之前所说，根据开发人员的经验和技能，将代码与外部依赖项正确分离并不容易。

所以在很多组织中，我们仍然看到大部分测试用例不得不依赖物理数据库进行测试。 但我不得不说这是不正确的。

在这篇文章中，我通过一个真实的例子来解释fast-check的用法以及它是如何接近实践的。 尽管如此，我希望我们不必再次面对这个问题，至少在阅读了我之前的文章之后，让我们尝试把那些不合理的测试用例翻过来。



原文:

The Pragmatic Programmer introduces a method of testing called property-based testing, in which an example is given in Python, using the framework hypothesis.

The usage of hypothesis is very intuitive and simple, and presents the concept of property-based testing perfectly. So I also wanted to find an equivalent alternative in Node. Two of them have high star ratings on Github, JSVerify with 1.6K stars and fast-check with 2.8K stars. So I took some time to study fast-check a little bit and try to get closer to my daily work.

This article is a recap, and a simple example to document the experience.

Why Property-Based Testing?

Before providing examples, let's explain why we use property-based tests. In fact, I don't like the term property-based. In my words, "extremely high-volume" testing.

We all know that Test Pyramid is as follows.

And in my previous article, I mentioned what’s difference between unit tests and integration tests. At the lower levels of the pyramid, the more test cases are required.

Even so, it is difficult to generate a large number of test cases. We usually write corresponding tests based on known conditions or product specifications, sometimes we may remember to write boundary tests (sometimes not), and sometimes we may rely on simple random verification of functionality, e.g. faker.

However, in general, even if we try hard to come up with test cases, we cannot cover all scenarios, and we call this testing method example-based testing. This is because the test cases we come up with are basically extended from a certain example and cannot cover all the unknown contexts nor can we test all the boundary conditions.

At this point, we would like to have a framework automatically generate enough scenarios (reasonable scenarios or not) to verify the code we write, and the test cases we write only need to ensure their "properties" are correct. This is the origin of property-based testing.

Nevertheless

The reality is that integration testing is approximately the same as unit testing.

I have worked in many organizations, from large national enterprises to small startups. Whether I am a developer or a mentor, from past experience, unit testing is about as relevant as integration testing.

For most developers, it is not an easy task to properly divide unit testing and integration testing. To be able to split test cases entirely they need to have the skills of design patterns, dependency injection, dependency inversion, etc. to be able to do it well. Therefore, most test environments are based on a specific test environment, such as using docker-compose to generate a one-time database and test data and test on it.

The documents of fast-check is written based on the standard of unit test, and it seems that only the verification boolean is provided, that is, fc.assert, so I took some time to research to write a test case close to daily use.

Generally I need several abilities.

  1. Be able to test async/await.
  2. Be able to verify more contexts, such as assertEqual.

fast-check Introduction

Before we start writing test cases, let's have a look at the basic usage of fast-check.

First, let's introduce the structure of fast-check.

  • Assertion (fc.assert)
  • Properties (fc.property or fc.asyncProperty)

The function of fc.assert is to verify that all the tests automatically generated by the properties are correct. The properties are needed to describe two important blocks.

Runner is the context to be tested, i.e., the target. On the other hand, the arbitraries are the input parameters of the target, which are automatically generated by the properties, and all we have to do is to provide rules for them, e.g., only integers.

The following is a simple example.

fc.assert( fc.property(fc.integer(), fc.integer(), (i, j) => { return i + j === add(i, j); })
);

The two fc.integer() are arbitraries, and the later anonymous function is the runner, which takes two arguments i and j, corresponding to the previous arbitraries. We want to verify whether the function add really sums the two arguments correctly, so the result of add should be consistent with +.

Let's review the two requirements we just mentioned.

  1. fast-check is able to test async/await, runner can be a promise, and fc.assert itself is also a promise.
  2. Although our test target is add, but a good integration with some conditions in the runner can make not only the effect of boolean.

fast-check Examples

Now let's come to a more practical example. Suppose I have a database table with money for each user.

user_id money
123 100
456 200
abc 9999
def 0

There is a function async function getMoney(limit) which will sort money in ascending order and also determine how much money to return based on the parameters.

Now we want to test this black box.

describe("fast-check test", () => { before(async () => { // generate 10 random records }); it("#1", async () => { const result = await getMoney(100); expect(result.length).to.be.equal(10); }); it("#2", async () => { await fc.assert( fc.asyncProperty(fc.integer(), async (i) => { const result = await getMoney(i); return result.length <= 10 && result.length >= 0; }) ); }); it("#3", async () => { await fc.assert( fc.asyncProperty(fc.integer({ min: 0, max: 10 }), async (i) => { const result = await getMoney(i); return result.length === i; }) ); }); it("#4", async () => { await fc.assert( fc.asyncProperty(fc.integer(), async (i) => { const result = await getMoney(i); if (result.length > 1) { let prev = parseFloat(result[0]); for (let i = 1; i < result.length; i++) { const curr = parseFloat(result[i]); if (curr < prev) { return false; } prev = curr; } } return true; }) ); });
});

Let me explain in brief.

  1. Just simply verify the function really works, there is no use of fast-check.
  2. Given an arbitrary integer, the length of the return result should be between 0 and 10, because we only created ten records in before.
  3. Given a range of integers, the length of the return should be equal to the given length.
  4. Verify the order of the whole array is indeed ascending. From this runner can be seen, even very complex conditions can be verified, but be careful not to make bugs in the test case resulting in the need for a test case of the test case.

If a problem is detected, fast-check will also tell you what kind of arbitraries it uses to detect the problem. For example,

Counterexample: [-1234567890]

This means the test case failed when i = -1234567890. It is possible the negative number is not handled correctly or the "large" negative number is not handled correctly. This is the time to write a real unit test (or integration test) and verify -1234567890, so that such a failed case can be used as a regression test afterwards.

Conclusion

Ideally, when testing database behavior like this, we would use techniques such as dependency injection to isolate the physical database in order to improve testing performance. But as I said earlier, it is not easy to properly separate code from external dependencies depending on the experience and skill of the developer.

So in many organizations, we still see that most of the test cases have to rely on the physical database for testing. But I have to say this is incorrect.

In this article, I explain the usage of fast-check through a real-life example and how it is close to practice. Nevertheless, I hope we don't have to face this again, at least after reading my previous article, let's try to turn over those unreasonable test cases.
item.author: lazypro

item.title: 数据库 | Databases
item.description:
什么是数据库？

数据库是数据的集合。 它被组织成结构信息，通常由数据库管理系统控制或称为 DBMS。 最常见的数据库类型通常以行和列的形式建模在一系列表中，以提高处理和数据查询的效率。 大多数数据库使用结构化查询语言 (SQL) 来编写和查询数据。

什么是结构化查询语言（SQL）？

数据存储在称为表的数据库对象中。 表是相关数据条目的集合，由列和行组成。

让我们有一个名为“客户”的表。 下面的例子是看“客户”表：

关键字“SELECT”表示访问下表。 所以总而言之，该语句是说我们选择查看表客户中的所有列。

每个表都被分解成更小的实体，称为字段。 字段是表中的一列，旨在包含有关表中每条记录的信息。 还有一行有时称为记录。 行是表中存在的每个单独条目。 想想客户表。 记录是表中的水平实体。 列是表中的垂直实体，包含表中特定字段内的所有信息。



原文:

What is a database?

A database is a collection of data. Its organized to structure information that is usually controlled by a database management system or refered to as DBMS. The most common type of database is typically modeled in rows and columns in. a series of tables to make processing and data query efficient. Most databases use Structured Query Language(SQL) for writing and querying data.

What is Structured Query Language(SQL)?

SQL is a programming language used by relational databases to query, manipulate, define data, and to provide access control. The data is stored in database objects that's called tables. A table is a collection of related data entries and it consists of columns and rows.

Let's have a table called Customers. The following example is to look at the "Customers" table:

The keyword "SELECT" means to get access to the following table. "*" means for all the columns, "FROM" keyword is to refer to which table you want and Customers is the table. So all together the statement is saying we select to see all the columns from the table Customers.

Every table is broken up into smaller entities called fields. A field is a column in a table that is designed to contain information about every record in the table. There is also a row sometimes refer to as a record. A row is each indivdual entry that exist in a table. Think in the Customers table. A record is a horizontal entity in a table. A column is a vertical entity in a table that contains all information within a specific field in a table.
item.author: rusty619

item.title: 如何为您的网站带来免费流量 | How to Drive Free Traffic to your Website
item.description:
杰西卡-阿戈耶

发表于拥有网站的人面临的一大挑战是为他们的网站增加流量。 您网站上的访问者数量根据网站的目标而有所不同，这可能是网站的目标是增加销售额、产生高质量的潜在客户、创造机会、提升品牌知名度、产生更多收入或达到志同道合的人，建立一个社区，理由是无限的。

因此，为您的网站带来流量至关重要，您可以拥有最有吸引力的网站，但是，如果您没有网站流量，那它只是一个空壳，一开始就违背了创建它的目的。 有几种方法可以为您的网站带来流量，其中包括： 使用社交媒体、推荐网站、付费搜索、自然搜索、电子邮件通讯、直接流量等。

任何网站的流量都可以提高知名度和参与度，目标是吸引高质量的访问者访问您的网站，这些访问者可能会转化为客户。 在本文中，我们将重点介绍免费为您的网站带来流量的不同有效方法。 让我们看看如何利用免费渠道被发现：

搜索引擎优化
这些搜索引擎旨在根据互联网用户提交的搜索查询收集信息。 呈现的结果称为 SERP（搜索引擎结果页面）。

在搜索引擎上，查询是在一行结果中，通常根据排名。 虽然搜索引擎的算法是独一无二的，并且使用各种复杂的算法来生成搜索结果，但这些算法的一个共同主题是它们采用网页的关键元素，如页面标题、描述标签、URL、内容和关键字密度的网页，从而将该页面放在它在 SERP 上排名的位置。 随着每天创建数百个网站和新网页，为您的网站带来流量的前两种方式之一是通过搜索引擎优化 [SEO]。

以下三种方法可以提高您在任何搜索引擎上的 SEO 排名：

  1. 关键字：页面搜索引擎优化的关键是关键字策略。 关键字是具有重要意义的词。 例如，如果您的网站是关于健康生活的，那么像健身、健康和养生之类的词是应该在您的网站上战略性地出现的关键词。 这些是您网站页面上的展示位置。 当有查询时，搜索引擎会与您网站上的内容进行交互，它们会显示为 SERP。 这就是为什么内容创建是我们的下一个重点。

  2. 搜索引擎优化内容：搜索引擎优化和内容创建齐头并进。 没有内容，SEO 就无法工作，反之亦然。 例如，众所周知，谷歌的搜索引擎算法非常关注新内容，这就是为什么有必要与您网站上的内容保持一致。 这是您可以使用的内容创建方法列表，这些方法可以帮助您进行 SEO。

  • 搜索引擎优化登陆页面很棒有几个原因。 典型的登录页面是为特定原因创建的独立网页。 例如，具有特别优惠促销的销售人员可以创建一个登录页面来宣传优惠。 但是，优化的 SEO 登陆页面适用于搜索引擎。 这意味着着陆页上的某些功能会吸引搜索引擎算法，这些算法决定了对搜索者的价值。
  • 专用博客：另一种不断创建内容的方法是维护博客。 无论您的网站是关于什么的，许多所有者都发现拥有一个博客功能，您可以在其中定期发布具有关键字的相关和新内容，这是在搜索引擎上排名的最佳方式之一。 您创建的内容和关键字越多，您开始在搜索引擎上排名并吸引访问您网站的访问者就越多。 通过共享常规内容，您可以在持续创建内容的前三个月内捕获流量并增长 100% 以上。
  • 评论和推荐是另一种吸引流量到您网站的方式。 它们是您网站的用户提供的内容。 收集推荐和评论是在您的网站上捕获关键字并吸引新访问者的一种形式。 例如，“这是顶级生活方式博客，我很高兴找到它……”这样的评论抓住了这个市场中两个排名靠前的关键词； 顶级生活方式博客和生活方式。 该算法还喜欢评论和推荐； 因此，您从访问者或客户那里收集的评论越多越好。

  • 网站优化：在建立网站时，您应该针对搜索引擎优化网站。 以谷歌搜索引擎为例，他们的算法优先级是显示匹配或最接近搜索查询的结果。 这就是为什么您必须在您的网站上优化某些关键区域的原因，它们包括：

     ** Title Tags** appear as a clickable headline for the search result when displayed as a search snippet on a search engine results page (SERP). The significance is why it should have a focus keyword. Here's a look at TechCabal's title tag - “**Leading Africa's tech conversation”**

    标题标签或标题标签将网页上的标题和副标题与页面内容的其余部分区分开来。 它们也是 HTML 标记，代表介绍性内容及其显示方式。 标题标签对于导航和可读性至关重要，用于改善网页的 SEO。 它们按重要性排序，从 H1 到 H6 文本格式，其中 H1 和 H2 是搜索引擎算法最重要的。 对话”在他们的标题标签中出现。

页面副本与您网站的顶级页面（如主页和关于我们页面）上的网站副本相同。 在这些页面上创建内容时，请确保适度使用关键字。 你的文案应该是有吸引力的，并且应该能够保留

用户连接到页面。

它是互联网上的一个地址，应该尽可能简单。 您的 URL 还可以作为突出关键字的机会，应使用小写字母

用连字符分隔的定义关键字。

这是来自 tech cabal 网站的页面示例：

 **Images** also count when you think of improving your SEO. It is important to use an alternative text(Alt text) on every picture you upload to your site. This is what appears when an image does not load, letting the user know what the image is. These tags help to give context when you use the image search on Google.

此外，在搜索引擎上，您可以使用图像和视频进行搜索。 这是始终正确标记它们的另一个原因。

 **Meta Description** provides a summary of a webpage. This HTML element is displayed as a part of the search engine results page (SERP) snippet and is used to give a user some context as to how the page relates to their query. Meta descriptions are the two lines of text that appear below a clickable link. Look at tech cabals, Meta description:

搜索引擎优化一开始似乎总是很有效。 但是，只要您为 SEO 优化网站、使用关键字并创建常规内容，只需花费很少的时间和精力，您的网站流量就会飞跃。 要跟踪进度，您可以在实施这些 SEO 策略之前对网站上的自然流量和关键字进行基准测试。 您可以从 SEO 工作中查看 2-4 个月的流量增长情况。

谷歌我的商家
此 Google 产品是免费的商家信息，可让您提供您的商家详细信息。 在此服务上创建您的企业资料是免费的，因为此网络工具的目标是帮助本地企业在谷歌搜索引擎上获得更高的知名度。

除了在 Google 我的业务工具上列出您的业务外，您还可以轻松地与目标受众建立联系，不仅是在搜索上，而且通过 Google 地图。 这样，当人们搜索与您提供的产品或服务相关的字词时，您的详细信息就会显示在 Google 搜索引擎的右侧。 但是你的网站呢？ 该工具如何帮助增加流量？

  • 该列表显示访问者如何访问您的网站，其中包含指向您网站的链接。
  • 它改善了您的网站搜索，也改善了您的本地搜索引擎优化。
  • 当人们使用 Google 地图搜索附近的商家时，更有可能出现本地商家的列表； 如果您经营一家小型企业，这是有益的。

优化的“Google 我的商家”网站将为您的业务提供帮助。 但是，获得良好结果的关键是完善您的个人资料并确保其得到全面优化。

电子邮件营销是一种直接营销，涉及向现有订阅者或潜在客户列表发送特定消息。 它使用个性化的群发电子邮件来宣传特别优惠、分享新产品发布或与订阅者建立关系以产生兴趣。 电子邮件营销的潜力是无限的，为您的网站带来流量也是其中的一部分。 如果您一直在通过联系表格、弹出式表格、注册表单或任何其他媒体收集电子邮件地址，那么发送时事通讯是一个不错的起点。

您还可以分享有关您的产品或服务的其他事件或新闻。 您应始终确保号召性用语仍然是“访问我们的网站”。 另外，请记住，电子邮件营销应该适度，因为人们可以轻松地取消订阅您的电子邮件。

电子邮件营销仍然是接触客户和潜在客户的最佳方式之一。 来宾发帖
最好的搜索引擎优化实践是访客发帖，也称为访客博客。 博客文章永远存在于网络上，只要内容仍然相关且可搜索，尤其是常青内容，就会保持相关性。

访客发帖是向另一个个人博客或网站投稿，以建立反向链接、曝光率和权威。 反向链接很重要，因为具有多个反向链接的页面往往具有更高的有机搜索。 就上下文而言，反向链接是从一个网站到另一个网站的链接，并且已被证明是提高搜索排名和促进有关您网站的对话的可靠方法。

访客发帖时，您应该注意那些允许您定位用户正在搜索的关键字的文章，这会为您的网站带来更多流量。

移动优化
访问者从移动设备访问您的网站以获得引人入胜的体验的过程称为移动搜索引擎优化 [Mobile SEO]。

移动优化是一个涉及重新设计网站以提高响应能力的过程。 大多数网站访问者使用他们的手机访问网络，这就是为什么移动优化已成为推动任何网站流量不可或缺的一部分。 优化网站的主要功能包括： 压缩图像以获得更快的加载时间，没有弹出窗口或至少易于关闭弹出窗口，简单，整洁和响应式设计。

移动搜索引擎优化总是考虑用户浏览网站的体验。 谷歌搜索引擎非常重视移动优化，如果您的网站没有经过移动优化，可能不会在搜索结果的第一页对您进行排名。

结论
上面列出的方法是您今天可以应用于网站的不同策略，以帮助您在不增加成本的情况下增加流量。 如果您目前正在建立一个网站或已经有一个网站但难以获得网站访问量，那么遵循这些推荐的步骤将大大帮助您增加网站访问量。

佩奇·尼德林豪斯 - May 15 5月15日



原文:

Jessica-Agorye

Posted on May 15

One big challenge that people who own a website face is driving traffic to their websites. The number of visitors you have on your website translates differently depending on the aim of the website, it could be that the goal of the website is to increase sales, generate quality leads, create opportunities, promote brand awareness, generate more revenue, or reach like-minded people and build a community, the reasons are limitless.

Therefore it is vital to drive traffic to your website, you can have the most attractive website, however, if you don't have website traffic, it's just an empty shell and defeats the purpose for creating it in the first place. There are several ways to drive traffic to your website, and they include; using social media, referral sites, paid search, organic search, email newsletters, direct traffic, and others.

Traffic to any website improves visibility and engagement, and the goal is to drive quality visitors to your site that are likely going to convert into customers. In this article, we’ll focus on different effective ways to drive traffic to your website for free. Let's look at how you can get discovered by utilizing free channels:

Search Engine Optimization
Search engines like Google, Yahoo, and MSN are used to gain website traffic. These search engines are designed to collect information based on search queries submitted by internet users. The results presented are called SERPs (search engine results pages).

On the search engine, queries are in a line of results, usually according to ranking. While the search engine’s algorithm is unique and uses varying and complex algorithms to generate search results, a common theme of these algorithms is that they take key elements of a web page like the page title, description tag, the URL, content, and keyword density of a webpage and thus put that page where it ranks on SERPs. With hundreds of websites and new web pages being created daily, among the top two ways to drive traffic to your site is through Search Engine Optimization [SEO].

SEO is one of the prime ways to increase website traffic for free. Here are three ways you can improve your SEO ranking on any of the search engines:

  1. Keywords: The key to on-page SEO is Keyword Strategy. A keyword is a word that carries great significance. For example, if your website is about healthy living, words like fitness, wholesomeness, and regimen are keywords that should appear strategically across your website. These are placements made across the pages on your website. When there's a query, the search engine interacts with the contents on your website, and they appear as SERPs. This is why content creation is our next point.

  2. SEO Content: SEO and Content Creation go hand in hand. SEO cannot work without content, and vice versa. For example, Google's search engine algorithm has been known to focus a lot on new content, and this is why it's necessary to be consistent with the content on your websites. Here's a list of content creation methods you can use that would help you with your SEO.

  • SEO Landing pages are great for several reasons. A typical landing page is a standalone webpage created for a specific reason. For example, a salesperson with a special offer promo can create a landing page to promote the offer. However, optimized SEO landing pages are for the search engine. This means that some features on the landing page appeal to search engine algorithms, which decide the value to searchers.
  • Dedicated Blog: Another way to constantly create content is by maintaining a Blog. Whatever your website is about, many owners have found that having a blog feature where you regularly post relevant and new content that features keywords is one of the best ways to rank on search engines. The more content and keywords you create, the more you begin to rank on the search engines and capture visitors to your website. By sharing regular content, you can capture traffic and grow by over 100% within your first three months of creating content consistently.
  • Reviews & Testimonials are another way to attract traffic to your website. They are content provided by users of your website. Gathering testimonials and reviews are a form of capturing keywords on your website and attracting new visitors. For example, "This is the top lifestyle blog, and I am glad I found it..." A review like this captures two high-ranking keywords in this market; Top lifestyle blog and Lifestyle. The algorithm also loves reviews and testimonials; therefore, the more you can gather reviews from your visitors or customers, the better.

  • Site Optimization: When building a website, you should optimize the site for search engines. Using the Google search engine as an example, their algorithm priority is to show results that match or most closely match the search query. This is why there are certain critical areas you must optimize on your website and they include:

    ** Title Tags** appear as a clickable headline for the 
search result when displayed as a search snippet on a 
search engine results page (SERP). The significance 
is why it should have a focus keyword. 
Here’s a look at TechCabal's title tag - “**Leading 
Africa’s tech conversation”**

    Header Tags or heading tags differentiate the headings and the subheadings on a webpage from the rest of the page's content. They are also HTML tags that - represent introductory content and how it should be displayed. A header tag is crucial for navigation and readability and is used to improve the SEO of a webpage. They rank in order of importance, from H1 to H6 text formatting, where H1 and H2 are the most essential for search engines algorithm. Tech Cabals H1 also includes the keyword “Leading Africa’s tech

    conversation” featured in their title tag.

Page Copy is the same as a website copy on your website's top-level pages like the home page and about us page. When creating content across these pages, ensure to use your keywords moderately. Your copy should be attractive and should be able to keep the

user hooked to the page.

** URL structure **contains a domain name, protocol, and path. It is an address on the internet and should be as simple as possible. Your URL also serves as an opportunity to highlight keywords and should be written in lower case with

defined keywords separated by hyphens.

Here's a sample of a page from the tech cabal website:

  **Images** also count when you think of improving your SEO. 
  It is important to use an alternative text(Alt text)  on 
  every picture you upload to your site. This is what appears 
  when an image does not load, letting the user know what the 
  image is. These tags help to give context when you use the 
  image search on Google.

Also, on search engines, you can search using images and videos. This is another reason to always label them correctly.

  **Meta Description** provides a summary of a webpage. This 
   HTML element is displayed as a part of the search engine 
   results page (SERP) snippet and is used to give a user some 
   context as to how the page relates to their query. Meta 
   descriptions are the two lines of text that appear below a 
   clickable link. Look at tech cabals, Meta description:

SEO always seems like work at first. However, with little time and effort, your website traffic would leap once you optimize your website for SEO, use keywords and create regular content. To track progress, you can benchmark organic traffic and keywords on your website before implementing these SEO strategies. You can check back between 2-4 months of traffic growth from your SEO efforts.

Google My Business
This product from Google is a free business listing that allows you to provide your business details. It is free to create your business profile on this service, as this web tool's goal is to help local businesses attain higher visibility on the google search engine.

Apart from listing your business on the Google my business tool, you can easily connect with your target audience, not just on search but through Google Maps. This way, when people search for terms related to the product or service you offer, your details show up on the right side of Google’s search engine. But what about your website? And how does this tool help drive traffic?

  • The listing shows visitors how to visit your website with a link to your website included.
  • It improves your website search and also improves your local SEO.
  • Listing for a local business is more likely to appear when people search for a nearby business using Google Maps; this is beneficial if you run a small business.

An optimized Google My Business website will help your business. However, the key to getting good results is to complete your profile and make sure it is fully optimized.

*Email Marketing *
Email marketing is a type of direct marketing that involves sending out specific messages to a list of already existing subscribers or potential clients. It uses personalized mass emails to promote special offers, share new product releases, or build relationships with subscribers to create interest. The potential of email marketing is limitless, and driving traffic to your website is also a part of it. If you have been collecting email addresses through contact forms, pop-up forms, sign-up forms, or any other medium, sending out newsletters is a great place to start.

You can also share other events or news about your product or service. You should always make sure that the call to action remains “visit our website.” Also, remember that email marketing should be moderate because people can easily unsubscribe from your emails.

Email marketing is still one of the best ways to reach clients and potential clients. Some examples of email marketing companies that run a free package are Mailchimp, Hubspot, and Omnisend.

Guest Posting
Among the best Search Engine Optimization practices is Guest Posting also referred to as guest blogging. Blog posts live perpetually on the web and remain relevant as long as the content is still relevant and searchable, especially the evergreen content.

Guest posting is contributing a post to another personal blog or website to build backlinks, exposure, and authority. Backlinks are significant as pages with several backlinks tend to have a higher organic search. For context, backlinks are links from one website to another website and have proven to be a surefire way to improve your search ranking and boost conversations about your website.

When guest posting, you should pay attention to articles that allow you to target the keywords your users are searching for, which drives more traffic to your website.

Mobile Optimization
A 2021 statistic stated that mobile devices generated 54.8% of global website traffic. The process of visitors accessing your website from mobile devices to have an engaging experience is known as Mobile Search Engine Optimization [Mobile SEO].

Mobile optimization is a process that involves redesigning a website to improve responsiveness. Most website visitors use their mobile phones to access the web and this is why mobile optimization has become an integral part of driving traffic to any website. The key features of an optimized site include; compressed images for faster load times, no pop-ups or at least easy to close pop-ups, simple, uncluttered, and responsive design.

Mobile SEO always considers the user’s experience of navigating the website. Google search engine takes mobile optimization seriously and may not rank you on the first page of the search results if your website is not mobile optimized.

Conclusion
The ways outlined above are different strategies you can apply to websites today to help you increase traffic at no additional cost. If you are currently in the middle of building a website or already have one but struggle to get traffic to your website, following these recommended steps will considerably help you drive traffic to your website.

Paige Niedringhaus - May 15
